---
title: "Path to AI / Machine Learning"
subtitle: "The mathematics that lets a machine learn from examples"
---

## What you can do at the end of this path

- Read and understand any modern ML paper: derivations of loss functions,
  gradient descent variants, backprop, attention mechanics.
- Implement neural networks from scratch and explain *why* each design choice
  matters.
- Reason about why a model is failing in terms of its loss landscape, its
  inductive bias, or its data.

## The path

ML stands on a tripod: linear algebra (representations), calculus
(optimisation), and probability (uncertainty and inference). All three start
much earlier than people realise.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Tier 1<br/>Ratio, average, variables] --> B[Tier 2<br/>Linear functions, systems]
    B --> C[Tier 3<br/>Combinatorics, basic probability]
    C --> D[Tier 4<br/>Calculus + vectors]
    D --> E[Tier 5<br/>Linear algebra<br/>Multivariable calculus<br/>Probability & statistics]
    E --> F[Tier 6<br/>Optimisation, info theory]
    F --> G[Tier 7 — AI/ML track:<br/>Statistical learning theory<br/>Manifold methods<br/>RL theory]

    classDef built fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    class A built
```

## Where to start right now

The ML path begins, quietly, with **Ratio & Proportion** in Tier 1 (every
neural network weight is, conceptually, a ratio between an input and an
output) and **Data & Average** (the very first statistic). Walk Tier 1 in
order; don't skip ahead.

## What's built / what's planned

- ✅ Tier 1: *What is a Number?* (exemplar lesson)
- 📋 The full Tier 1 chain feeding into ML is planned but not yet authored.
